serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup is approximately 400 g, providing around 150 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table — Tom Yum Soup with Prawns (estimated from component analysis); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy shrimp broth and prawn entries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for 'Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup' as a composite dish. Values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA component data for prawns, lemongrass broth, and aromatics, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for shrimp (raw) and clear broth. The input record's per-100g values (energy 37.5 kcal, protein 3.75 g, fat 1.0 g, carbohydrate 2.5 g) are consistent with this estimation and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients are set to -1 where no reliable composite data is available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on clear vegetable-prawn broth soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup. GI estimated at 38 (low) based on the closest analogous food: clear vegetable and seafood broth soups with minimal starch, as referenced in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care). Carbohydrate content is only 2.5 g/100g; glycemic load per 400 g serving = (38 × 10.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.8, confirming negligible glycemic impact.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g from fish sauce and broth
hypertension.guidance: A 400 g bowl delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, which represents 52–60% of the recommended daily limit for hypertensive individuals (2000–2300 mg/day per WHO/DASH guidelines); patients should request reduced fish sauce, use low-sodium broth, and avoid adding extra condiments at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.5 g/100g) and low GI (~38) with protein from prawns slowing gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup has minimal glycemic impact with an estimated GI of 38 and a glycemic load of approximately 3.8 per 400 g serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added rice or noodles, consistent with ADA dietary guidance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load (300 mg/100g) and dietary cholesterol (37.5 mg/100g) from prawns, partially offset by low saturated fat (0.25 g/100g) and anti-inflammatory aromatics
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (0.25 g/100g) and anti-inflammatory polyphenols from lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves are cardioprotective, but the high sodium load (approximately 1200 mg per bowl) warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; reduced-sodium preparation is strongly recommended per AHA guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory aromatics (lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves) and vitamin C from lime juice, with nightshade caution for chilli
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The aromatic herbs in Tom Yum provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols and vitamin C (7.5 mg/100g) that may benefit RA management, but individuals with nightshade sensitivity should monitor for joint flare responses to chilli, and the sodium load (300 mg/100g) may worsen RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup (ต้มยำกุ้งใส; 冬阴虾汤) is a classic Thai clear-broth soup featuring succulent whole prawns simmered in an aromatic stock infused with lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, fish sauce, lime juice, and chilli padi. Unlike the creamy coconut-milk variant (Tom Kha), this clear version relies entirely on the herb-and-spice broth for its characteristic bold, spicy, and sour flavour profile. The soup is typically garnished with fresh coriander and straw mushrooms. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 37.5 kcal, 3.75 g protein, 1.0 g fat, and only 2.5 g carbohydrate, making it a low-calorie, low-glycaemic dish. Sodium is notable at 300 mg/100g due to fish sauce and broth, yielding approximately 1200 mg per standard 400 g bowl. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Clinically, it is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its very low carbohydrate content and estimated GI of 38, but requires sodium moderation for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. Anti-inflammatory aromatics including lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves contribute polyphenols relevant to inflammatory conditions. Vitamin C from lime juice (7.5 mg/100g) provides antioxidant support. This dish is widely served in Singapore's Thai restaurants and hawker stalls as a standalone soup or as part of a shared Thai meal.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA component data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for prawns and clear broth; no single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish as prepared. Values are internally consistent and plausible for a clear prawn soup of this composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for clear seafood soups.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this composite clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup are not available as a single entry in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible for a clear prawn broth with no hydrogenated fats). This is a Thai dish served in Singapore; the mandatory Thai-script local name (ต้มยำกุ้งใส) is provided. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable as this is a Thai-cuisine dish, not a Singaporean-origin dish, and the G6 multilingual rule requires Thai-script for Thai dishes. The input record listed country_of_origin as Singapore (reflecting the serving context via GrabFood), but the dish's culinary origin is Thailand; region_of_origin is set to Thailand accordingly. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for Clear Tom Yum Prawn Soup is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026.
